{"society_power": 8.0, "state_power": 7.5, "society_change": 0.0, "state_change": 0.5, "key_event": "Church-state separation and Dreyfus resolution consolidate secular republic", "reasoning": "The Combes-era secularization drive, Law on Separation (1905), Ministry of Labor, and colonial consolidation modestly expanded state administrative and coercive reach; society remained strong and vigorous (mass press, CGT, Dreyfusard victory) but its gains from labor mobilization and Dreyfus exoneration were offset by Clemenceau's strike repression and the weakening of Church-based networks, leaving it roughly stable."}
